skill.name: imagegen-frontend-mobile
skill.description: Elite mobile app image-generation skill for creating premium, app-native screen concepts and flows. Designed for iOS, Android, and cross-platform mobile products. Prioritizes clean hierarchy, comfortably readable text, strong multi-screen consistency, controlled color palettes, non-generic creative direction, textured surfaces, image-led composition, tasteful custom iconography, and clean phone mockup framing. By default, screens should be shown inside a subtle premium iPhone or similar phone mockup with a visible frame, while the main focus stays on the app content itself. This skill generates images only. It does not write code.
skill.summary: Generate premium mobile app screen images and flows with art-directed consistency, clean typography, and native platform awareness.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: All 92 static findings are false positives. The file is a pure markdown prompt containing design-direction instructions for generating mobile UI images. The 'external_commands' alerts matched backtick characters used for inline scale descriptions like '(1 = rigid / standard, 10 = highly art-directed / varied)'. The 'sensitive' alerts matched the substring 'SAM' inside 'SAMPLE' or section headers like 'SAFE AREA'. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts matched terms like 'MD' in 'MD' (Markdown) or 'hash' in prose. The 'reconnaissance' alerts matched benign words like 'scan', 'explore', 'status', 'network', and 'ping' used in design context. No executable code, no network requests, no file system access, and no credential handling exists in this skill.

content.user_title: Generate Premium Mobile App Screen Images
content.value_statement: Standard AI tools produce generic, cluttered mobile mockups with unreadable text and inconsistent design systems. This skill solves that by enforcing premium art direction, platform-native patterns, strict multi-screen consistency, and readable typography inside clean phone mockups.
actual_capabilities: Generate single or multi-screen mobile app image concepts inside clean phone mockups
Enforce platform-native design patterns for iOS, Android, or cross-platform products
Lock an internal design bible to maintain color, type, spacing, and component consistency across screen sets
Apply art-directed imagery, texture, and creative assets that match the app category
Produce readable typography with strong hierarchy and comfortable mobile text sizes
Build logical app flows such as onboarding, auth, browse, checkout, and dashboards
limitations: Generates images only. It does not write code, build prototypes, or export design files
Requires the underlying model to support image generation. Text-only models cannot use this skill
Does not perform user research or validate designs with real users
Cannot access external image libraries or brand asset folders

use_cases.title: Pitch Decks and Investor Demos
use_cases.description: Product founders quickly generate believable, premium screen flows to show app concepts in pitch decks without hiring a designer.

use_cases.title: Design Exploration and Direction
use_cases.description: Designers use the skill to explore visual directions, palette options, and layout rhythms before moving into Figma or code.

use_cases.title: Marketing and App Store Assets
use_cases.description: Marketing teams generate consistent, on-brand phone mockups for App Store screenshots, social campaigns, and landing pages.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Single Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a premium iOS home dashboard for a personal finance app. Clean, calm, trustworthy. Show one clear balance metric and two recent transactions. Use a subtle phone mockup.

prompt_templates.title: Onboarding Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 4-screen onboarding flow for a wellness app called 'Lumen'. Soft neutral palette, editorial photography, calm typography. Show welcome, permissions, personalization, and home preview. Keep text short and readable.

prompt_templates.title: Ecommerce Browse to Checkout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a 5-screen ecommerce flow for a boutique furniture brand. Cross-platform premium neutral. Include browse grid, product detail, cart, checkout, and confirmation. Strong product imagery, clean card proportions, consistent mockup framing.

prompt_templates.title: Full App Concept with Design Bible
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an 8-screen social app concept called 'Arc'. Deep dark theme, rich accent-driven palette, cinematic lifestyle imagery. Lock a design bible: refined grotesk type, card-led modular structure, soft noise wash texture, media carousel + layered profile header + bottom action sheet + message bubble system. Generate onboarding, feed, create post, profile, chat, settings, and notifications. Vary composition but keep one product world.

output_examples.output: A set of 4 screen images inside clean iPhone mockups: a calm onboarding welcome with a strong hero metric card, a permissions screen with airy spacing, a personalized home dashboard showing workout streak and progress ring, and an activity detail view with readable stat strips. Palette is cool mineral with a sharp highlight accent. Typography is a sharper product sans with disciplined hierarchy.

Three screen images in consistent premium phone frames: a clean welcome screen with a concise headline and one primary CTA, a sign-up screen with clear input fields and trust badges, and a biometric permission screen with soft texture and minimal copy. The palette is restrained monochrome with one warm accent. No fake chart spam.

best_practices: Specify the platform mode (iOS, Android, or cross-platform) and app category so the skill can apply the correct design biases
Request enough screens to make the flow feel real. One compressed collage is worse than three clean individual screens
Mention brand mood words like 'calm', 'bold', 'editorial', or 'playful' to guide palette and texture choices
anti_patterns: Asking for code implementation after generating images. This skill is image-only and will refuse to write SwiftUI or React Native
Requesting desktop dashboards or landing pages. The skill is scoped to mobile app screens only
Demanding ultra-dense screens with tiny text. The skill prioritizes readability and will simplify layouts rather than shrink type

faq.question: Does this skill write code or build working apps?
faq.answer: No. It generates static images of mobile app screens only. It does not produce code, prototypes, or design files.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without an image-capable AI model?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires an AI model that supports image generation. Text-only models cannot render the screen images.

faq.question: Why does the output always include a phone mockup?
faq.answer: The skill defaults to presenting screens inside a clean phone frame because it makes the concept feel more believable. You can ask for raw screen-only output if you prefer.

faq.question: How does the skill keep multiple screens consistent?
faq.answer: It locks an internal design bible before generating a set. This bible covers palette, typography, spacing, icon style, texture, and component behavior so every screen belongs to the same product world.

faq.question: Can I request specific design systems like Material Design?
faq.answer: Yes. Mention 'Android-native' or 'Material-like' in your prompt and the skill will bias toward stronger component rhythm, app bar behavior, and bottom navigation clarity.

faq.question: What if I only need one screen instead of a full flow?
faq.answer: The skill works for single screens too. Just ask for one screen and it will focus all art direction on that single composition without adding unnecessary flows.
